article_title: Angular 22 Makes Signal Forms and Angular Aria Stable, Defaults New Components to OnPush, and Requires TypeScript 6
summary: Every feature claim, quote, and the June 3 2026 release date verified verbatim against the official Angular CHANGELOG v22.0.0 and angular.dev primary sources; only a non-blocking allowlist warning remains.

content_preview.title: Angular 22 Makes Signal Forms and Angular Aria Stable, Defaults New Components to OnPush, and Requires TypeScript 6
content_preview.summary: Angular's June 3 release graduates Signal Forms, Asynchronous Signals, and Angular Aria to stable, flips the default change-detection strategy to OnPush, and ships with Vitest as the default test runner.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Angular team released version 22 of its web framework on June 3, 2026, according to the project's [official v22 event page](https://angular.dev/events/v22), whose changelog reference marks the release as `2200-2026-06-03`. The update consolidates several reactivity features that had spent prior cycles in experimental or developer-preview status, promoting them to stable, and changes a long-standing default in how components detect and render updates.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (481 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure is appropriate. Technical depth is accurate and accessible; opt-out APIs (ChangeDetectionStrategy.Eager, provideHttpClient(withXhr)) are correctly contextualized.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 source snapshots read from disk; sha256 of each decompressed snapshot matches manifest.json (source-0 ebdf1f06, source-1 5c05bfbb, source-2 de62c47c). source-0 (angular.dev/events/v22, 200): release marker '2200-2026-06-03' present, and the quote 'Signal Forms, Asynchronous Signals, and Angular Aria are now stable, offering a production-ready reactive foundation' is verbatim. source-1 (raw CHANGELOG.md, 200): v22.0.0 header is '# 22.0.0 (2026-06-03)'; 'graduate signal forms APIs to public API', the OnPush-default sentence with ChangeDetectionStrategy.Eager, the provideHttpClient(withXhr) sentence, and 'TypeScript versions older than 6.0 are no longer supported' all appear verbatim under the v22.0.0 entry. source-2 (angular.dev/guide/testing, 200): 'covers the default testing setup for new Angular CLI projects, which uses Vitest' is verbatim and @angular/build:unit-test is present. No source was fetched via Archive.org fallback; no snapshot failed. The bot-blocked Medium-redirecting blog post is NOT cited in the body or in article.sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every feature specific (Signal Forms / Asynchronous Signals / Angular Aria stable; OnPush default for new components; TypeScript 6 floor; Vitest default test runner; Fetch-default HttpClient) and the June 3, 2026 release date trace to the official Angular CHANGELOG v22.0.0 and angular.dev primary publications, satisfying Rule 9. All quoted tokens verbatim. No fabricated specifics.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The 'line in the sand' framing in Analysis is interpretive but clearly labeled as analysis and not sensationalist. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior Angular coverage in the archive. Not a duplicate.
editor_notes.provenance_check: No orphan URLs: all three body-linked URLs are present in article.sources, and all three sources are referenced in the body. Signature valid, hash valid.
recommendations: Two cited URLs (angular.dev/events/v22, angular.dev/guide/testing) are not on config/source_allowlist.txt — non-blocking warning only. angular.dev is the official Angular project domain and a reputable primary source; consider adding it to the allowlist. Per operating rules the allowlist file was NOT modified during this review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. All claims, quotes, specifics, and the release date verified verbatim against the primary Angular sources. The only finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning, which does not warrant a corrections record (no factual error or misattribution exists). High-quality submission ready for publication.
